title: Volumetric Weight Calculator
description: Industrial-grade volumetric weight calculation tool compliant with ISO 9001, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC standards. Calculates dimensional weight, actual weight, chargeable weight, and provides cost optimization insights.

inputs.label: Length (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Physical length of the package in centimeters. ISO 1:2016 standard for geometric product specifications.

inputs.label: Width (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Physical width of the package in centimeters.

inputs.label: Height (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Physical height of the package in centimeters.

inputs.label: Actual Weight (kg)
inputs.businessContext: Gross weight of the package including packaging. Per ISO 80000-4:2006.

inputs.label: Carrier DIM Factor
inputs.businessContext: Volumetric divisor used by carrier (e.g., FedEx/UPS: 5000, DHL: 5000, Air freight: 6000). WERC standard.

inputs.label: Package Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of packaging influences dimensional stability and handling. Lean packaging standard.

inputs.label: Stackable
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if package can be stacked. Affects storage density and handling cost. Six Sigma material flow optimization.

inputs.label: Hazardous Material
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if contents are hazardous (IMO/ADR/IATA). Affects surcharges and handling restrictions.

rules.message: All dimensions must be greater than zero.
Actual weight must be greater than zero.
No single dimension may exceed 500 cm (ISO 1:2016).
For cylinders, width and height must be equal (diameter).
Pallet dimensions must be at least 80x100x100 cm (EUR pallet standard).

thresholds.message: Package exceeds standard oversize threshold (68 kg DIM). Additional handling fees may apply.
DIM weight is more than 3x actual weight. Significant cost inefficiency detected. Consider repackaging.
Hazardous material surcharge will be applied. Ensure proper documentation (IATA/IMDG).
Non-stackable packages may incur additional storage and handling costs.

formulas.name: Package Volume (cm³)
formulas.description: Calculates the geometric volume of the package based on type.
formulas.formula: CASE WHEN package_type = 'cylinder' THEN PI() * (width_cm/2)^2 * length_cm ELSE length_cm * width_cm * height_cm END

formulas.name: Package Volume (m³)
formulas.description: Converts volume to cubic meters for freight billing.

formulas.name: Volumetric Weight (kg)
formulas.description: Dimensional weight based on carrier DIM factor. ISO 80000-4 compliant.

formulas.name: Chargeable Weight (kg)
formulas.description: The greater of actual weight and volumetric weight. Standard industry practice.

formulas.name: DIM Weight Ratio
formulas.description: Ratio of volumetric weight to actual weight. Key Lean metric for packaging efficiency.

formulas.name: Package Density (kg/m³)
formulas.description: Actual weight per cubic meter. Used for freight classification.

formulas.name: Estimated Shipping Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Base cost estimate using chargeable weight and a standard rate. Premium feature for real rates.
formulas.formula: chargeable_weight_kg * 2.50 + (CASE WHEN hazardous_material THEN 50 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN is_stackable = false THEN 15 ELSE 0 END)

primary.label: Chargeable Weight (kg)
primary.description: Final weight used for billing. Equals max(actual weight, volumetric weight).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Void Fill
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Ratio of empty space in packaging. High void fill increases DIM weight without adding value.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Non-Standard Packaging
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Use of non-standard box sizes leads to inefficient cube utilization.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Overpackaging
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Excessive packaging material adds weight and volume without protection benefit.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Carrier DIM Factor Mismatch
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Using a carrier with a lower DIM factor than optimal for the package density.

suggestedActions.action: Reduce package dimensions by 10% to lower volumetric weight. Consider custom box sizes.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Up to 15% reduction in shipping cost

suggestedActions.action: Evaluate alternative carriers with higher DIM factors (e.g., 6000 vs 5000) for dense packages.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Up to 20% reduction in chargeable weight

suggestedActions.action: Redesign packaging to be stackable. Use uniform box sizes and reinforced corners.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Reduced storage cost and handling surcharges

suggestedActions.action: Use adjustable packaging or void fill audit to reduce empty space below 20%.
Consolidate multiple items into one package to improve density and reduce per-unit cost.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: Up to 30% cost reduction per shipment
